summary: Iteration 29 verified: 18/18 backend tests + 2/2 frontend flows PASS. Notif defaults disabled, verify auto-provision, voice/transcribe 400+rate-limit, custom slugified expense category (backend + UI), web receipt upload (400/415/200/429), security headers, CORS via ASGI, payload guard 413, robots/sitemap/JSON-LD, /blog listing + built-with-emergent article. One critical code-level issue surfaced: /api/expenses/upload-receipt returns 502 to the client when the AI parser gives up (e.g., unrecognisable image). Envelope test would-be OK per spec but real users will see a hard error.

critical.issue: When ai_json exhausts its 3 retries (any upstream failure — tiny/unreadable image, quota, timeout) it raises HTTPException(502). upload_receipt does `except HTTPException: raise`, so the whole request fails with 502 even though the review spec explicitly says the expense should still be created with a blank vendor. Also breaks the rate-limit UX (user retries, keeps hitting 502). Fix: replace `except HTTPException: raise` with catching HTTPException where detail hints AI/parse and falling through to `parsed = {}` — OR call an internal `_ai_json_safe` that returns {} on failure.

minor.issue: Preview ingress overrides `access-control-allow-origin` to `*` for every response, masking the app-level CORS allow-list. The FastAPI middleware is configured correctly (verified in-process via TestClient), but any real-browser check against the preview URL will see `*`. Non-blocking for production karyaai.app if that ingress does not override — please verify prod ingress config.
Preview ingress terminates requests with a bogus Content-Length before hitting FastAPI, so end-to-end 413 cannot be tested via the public URL. Middleware itself works (verified in-process).

action_items: Fix upload_receipt 502 leak: when AI parsing fails (HTTPException 502 from ai_json), swallow it and create the expense with empty vendor/amount/items so the user still gets a stored receipt attachment. Do NOT swallow the two intentional HTTPExceptions (415 unsupported type, 422 unreadable PDF).
Verify production ingress (karyaai.app) does NOT rewrite access-control-allow-origin — otherwise the tightened app-level CORS is inert on the public surface.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 3,683 lines — long past the 700-line threshold. Recommend splitting into routers (auth, expenses, telegram, compliance, verify, security_middleware).

upload_receipt swallows generic Exception but re-raises HTTPException; ai_json raising 502 short-circuits an otherwise-successful upload. This pattern is copy-pasted from the Telegram receipt flow (line 1443 area) which has the same latent issue.
Rate limiter is in-process only (dict + asyncio.Lock). Fine for single-pod; a comment already flags this. Consider Redis before scaling horizontally.
test_credentials: Bearer test_session_karya1 (user test-user-karya1, DB karya_db_v2). Unchanged.
seed_data_creation: None persistent. Tests create+cleanup TEST_CUSTOM_CAT / TEST_UI_ITER29 expenses and one throwaway web-upload receipt (deleted at end).
context_for_next_testing_agent: CORS + payload-guard cannot be black-box tested against the preview ingress (it strips/rewrites headers). Use starlette TestClient against server.app for those two. For upload-receipt tests, use PIL to draw a real-looking receipt (>= 400x300 with text) — a 1x1 pixel PNG makes OpenAI vision reject the image and expose the 502 bug. Verify SID auto-provisioning already ran; system_config now contains 'twilio_verify_sid'.
rca of the issue: upload_receipt (server.py:2910) explicitly re-raises HTTPException on the AI parse step. Since ai_json() at server.py:174 raises HTTPException(502) after 3 retries, any client-side upload whose image the vision model rejects (or upstream is briefly down) fails hard instead of degrading to an unparsed receipt. Reproduction: POST a 1x1 PNG to /api/expenses/upload-receipt → 502 in ~8s (3 gpt-4o attempts). Expected: 200 with empty vendor/amount + expense.attachment.path set.
